---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    grid:
      margin-width: 180px
      body-width: 780px
    theme: cosmo
---

<style>
.project-block {
  padding: 1.5rem 0;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #e9ecef;
}

.project-title {
  margin-top: 0;
}

.section-header {
  margin-top: 3rem;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}

img {
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 6px 18px rgba(0,0,0,0.12);
}
</style>


## Drunk Driving Surveillance Dashboard

::: {.project-block}
::: {.grid .align-items-center}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

**Overview:**  
The Drunk Driving Surveillance Dashboard is a publicly available tool designed to monitor alcohol-related crashes in El Paso. It includes over 30 indicators covering crash trends, geographic patterns, and driver and vehicle characteristics. The dashboard is updated monthly, enabling near real-time monitoring of impaired driving in the city.

<br>

<a href="crash_dashboard.html" target="_blank" class="btn btn-primary">
View Dashboard
</a>

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

![](screenshot of dashboard.jpg)

:::
:::
:::

---

## In the Media

::: {.project-block}
::: {.grid .align-items-center}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

**Opinion Article:**  
This article explores why arrest data can be misleading when trying to understand drunk driving trends and why researchers often rely on alternative measures instead. This article also includes a line graph comparing the rate of alcohol-involved crashes to other Texas cities.

<br>

<a href="https://elpasomatters.org/2026/02/25/opinion-why-el-paso-needs-better-data-to-solve-drunk-driving-problem/" target="_blank" class="btn btn-primary">
Read Article
</a>

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

![](opinion_article.jpg)

:::
:::
:::

---

## Working Papers & Reports

---

### Does Public Shaming Deter Drunk Driving?

::: {.project-block}
::: {.grid .align-items-center}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

**Summary:**  
This study evaluates the impact of a social media campaign in El Paso that publicly shared booking photos of individuals arrested for driving while intoxicated (DWI). Using Bayesian structural time-series methods and multiple comparison groups, the analysis attempts to isolate the campaign’s effect on alcohol-related crashes.

The findings highlight both the potential and limits of public-facing deterrence strategies.

<br>

<a href="dwi_study.pdf" download class="btn btn-primary">
Download Paper (PDF)
</a>

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

![](dwi_study.jpg)

:::
:::
:::
